title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
content: “All my means are sane, my motive and my object mad.”
action: Acknowledge the calculated detachment from rationality to pursue a singular objective.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
content: “The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts. Dip it therefore in the dye of such thoughts as these: that wherever a man can live, there he can also live well.”
action: Prioritize internal state to determine environmental quality.

title: The Republic
author: Plato
content: “And may we not say that the most gifted minds, when they are ill-educated, become practically the worst?”
action: Implement structural guidance to manage high-level intellectual capability.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
content: “Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a large intelligence and a deep heart. The really great men must, I think, have great sadness on earth.”
action: Accept burden as an inherent cost of high consciousness.

title: Walden
author: Henry David Thoreau
content: “I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately, to front only the essential facts of life, and see if I could not learn what it had to live, and not, when I came to die, discover that I had not lived.”
action: Isolate yourself to conduct objective analysis of your life essentials.
